name: Conservation Laws
summary: Physical quantities (energy, momentum, charge, angular momentum) that remain constant in a closed system — the most powerful problem-solving tools in physics.
whenToUseIt: Use conservation laws whenever a system evolves over time and you want to relate the initial state to the final state without tracking every intermediate step.
steps: Identify the system and check whether it is closed (no external forces/inputs)
Identify which quantities are conserved (energy, momentum, angular momentum, charge)
Write the conservation equation: initial total = final total
Substitute known values and solve for unknowns
Check whether the answer is physically reasonable
example: Two ice skaters push apart from rest. Conservation of momentum: total momentum before = 0, so total momentum after = 0. If skater A (60 kg) moves at 2 m/s, skater B (80 kg) must move at -1.5 m/s in the opposite direction. No need to know the forces involved.
